title: Pyrus lochengensis
taxonomy: Pyrus lochengensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This family includes a wide variety of plants such as roses, apples, and pears. The genus Pyrus, which includes Pyrus lochengensis, is a group of deciduous trees and shrubs commonly known as wild pears. The classification of Pyrus lochengensis places it within the order Rosales, which also includes other flowering plants like the rose family. Further classification identifies Pyrus lochengensis as a member of the subfamily Maloideae.
uses: Pyrus lochengensis is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China. Research on its medicinal uses is limited, but it has been reported to have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. parts: Pyrus lochengensis bark, leaves, and fruits are utilized in traditional Chinese medicine. The bark of this plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism.
